# callbag-recharge
> State that flows. Reactive state management for TypeScript.

## What it does
Reactive state management with 5 primitives: state, derived, effect, producer, operator.
Glitch-free diamond resolution via two-phase push. 60+ tree-shakeable operators (map, filter, debounce, switchMap, etc.).
Framework-agnostic. Works frontend, backend, edge. ~1.1 KB core ESM, zero dependencies.

## When to use
- Simple state management (like Zustand/Jotai but framework-agnostic, no providers)
- Streaming data (LLM chunks, WebSocket, SSE) flowing into state
- Cancellable async operations (switchMap auto-cancels previous)
- Derived/computed values that are always consistent (diamond-safe, cached)
- Agentic workflows (session state, tool call lifecycle, multi-agent coordination)
- Event pipelines with 60+ operators for transform, buffer, window, error handling
- Full-stack TypeScript — same API for frontend state and backend streams

## Core API (5 primitives)
- `state(initial)` — readable/writable store. `.get()`, `.set(value)`, `.update(fn)`, `.source` (callbag)
- `derived([deps], fn)` — cached computed store, recomputes when deps change. `.get()`, `.source`
- `effect([deps], fn)` — side-effect, runs when deps change. Returns dispose function
- `producer(fn?, opts?)` — push-based source with cleanup. Actions: `.emit(value)`, `.complete()`, `.error(err)`. Options: `initial`, `equals`, `resetOnTeardown`, `resubscribable`
- `operator([deps], init, handler)` — custom transform node. Handler receives `(depIndex, type, data)`

## Composition
- `pipe(source, op1, op2, ...)` — compose operators into inspectable store chain
- `batch(fn)` — defer emissions until outermost batch ends (DIRTY signals still propagate immediately)
- `pipeRaw(source, fn1, fn2, ...)` — fuse transforms into single derived for ~2x throughput. `SKIP` sentinel for filter semantics

## Operators (import from callbag-recharge/extra)

Sources: interval, fromIter, fromAsyncIter, fromEvent, fromPromise, fromObs, of, empty, throwError, never
Filtering: filter, take, skip, first, last, find, elementAt, distinctUntilChanged, takeUntil
Transformation: map, scan, pairwise, startWith, flat, switchMap, concatMap, exhaustMap, groupBy
Combination: merge, combine, concat, race, withLatestFrom, partition
Time: debounce, throttle, delay, timeout, sample, audit
Buffering: buffer, bufferCount, bufferTime
Windowing: window, windowCount, windowTime
Aggregation: reduce, toArray
Error handling: rescue, retry, repeat
Utilities: tap, share, remember, subject, wrap
Sinks: forEach, subscribe

## Inspectability
- `Inspector.inspect(store)` — returns `{ name, kind, value, status }` for any store
- `Inspector.graph()` — returns Map of all named stores and their current state
- Signal hooks: `Inspector.onEmit`, `Inspector.onSignal`, `Inspector.onStatus`, `Inspector.onEnd`
- `Inspector.registerEdge(source, sink)` — track dependency graph
- Zero per-store cost: uses WeakMaps, opt-in only

## Install
```
npm install callbag-recharge
```

## Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/Callbag-Recharge/callbag-recharge
- Architecture: docs/architecture-v4.md
- Benchmarks: docs/benchmarks.md
